title: echinops ritro subsp. sartorianus
intro: Echinops ritro subsp. sartorianus, also known as the Globe Thistle, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its distinctive globe-shaped purple flowers. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Echinops ritro subsp. sartorianus include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory congestion, and supporting the immune system, while also exhibiting potential antioxidant and antibacterial effects. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to make teas, syrups, and salves that can be used to support respiratory health and reduce inflammation.
